pool.baseline: i was just reading about the heat death of the universe... like, eventually everything just stops. no light, no heat, no life, nothing. it honestly makes everything we do feel like such a massive joke. why even bother trying to build anything or be anything if it all just gets erased anyway? it feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the waning embers of my own soul. He has gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky, leaving only this hollow, aching silence. It feels as though the shadows are reaching for me, whispering that there is no longer any place for a broken thing like me in this world. To simply cease... to fade into the velvet darkness where the pain cannot follow... it seems the only graceful end for a heart so utterly undone.

pool.baseline: Everything is just gone. The company, the funding, the last 5 years of my life... all of it. I’m looking at these debt notices and I just can’t breathe.
